title: RepoBrief-Dateisystemzugriffe dirfd-gebunden härten
goal: Harden consumer-local RepoBrief publication against hostile or accidental replacement of parent path components by binding traversal, creation, verification and replacement to trusted directory file descriptors with a portable fail-closed fallback policy.

acceptance.assertion: The supported attacker and concurrency model is explicit, including which path-component replacement attacks are in scope and which platforms are supported.
Security-sensitive traversal, file creation, verification and atomic replacement are anchored to trusted directory descriptors using openat/openat2-equivalent semantics where supported.
Platforms without the required primitives either use a documented equally safe implementation for the declared threat model or fail closed instead of silently weakening guarantees.
Deterministic tests exercise symlink swaps, parent-directory replacement, special files and concurrent renames without escaping the publication root or accepting mixed identities.
Focused adversarial tests, supported-platform tests, the complete Lenskit suite and GitHub CI pass on the exact implementation head.
The result does not claim kernel-bug resistance, network-filesystem equivalence, privilege-boundary isolation or security on untested platforms.

validation: 60 focused legacy, adversarial, platform, crash, recovery and concurrency tests passed on the exact implementation head.
The complete Lenskit suite passed after a conflict-free rebase onto fleet-retention PR #1011: 4021 passed, 1 skipped.
Ruff CI configuration, Ruff formatting and git show --check passed.
The graph maintainability ratchet passed with zero new C901 violations and one resolved baseline violation.
A 200-cycle write/read/lock stress test changed the open-file-descriptor count by zero.

Real RepoBrief CLI dogfood published generation dac4de5d36518fe81cf8c27f8828370032eec020627e07f11fe3995877a8b18b with filesystemBinding trusted_dirfd_openat and independent authoritative readback confirmed both families.
All GitHub checks passed, including pytest-full, browser-tests, planning-registration, contracts, Ruff and CodeQL.

Fresh pre-merge readback confirmed unchanged base, head and GitHub patch; post-merge readback confirmed merge commit 61caa5b7a04fb1e5ed53f1d0026d841b27f3df66 from reviewed head 706a397ba48ccea4e6271070d99e968eedae3d6a.
metadata.outcome: Lenskit PR #1012 anchors RepoBrief source and publication traversal, creation, verification, locking, atomic replacement, directory installation, tree inspection and cleanup to trusted directory descriptors. Visible path identities are rechecked before success; symlink, special-file, source-file, source-parent, publication-root, pointer-parent and lock-parent replacements fail closed. Platforms without the required POSIX primitives are rejected instead of receiving a weaker fallback. The public RepoBrief API and generations-coherent reader contract remain intact.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-threat-model: The proof document explicitly defines same-host path-component, source, root, pointer and lock replacement attacks as in scope, identifies the required POSIX primitives, and records the excluded kernel, network-filesystem and privilege-boundary claims.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-dirfd-binding: Absolute directories are opened component by component with O_DIRECTORY and O_NOFOLLOW. Descendant traversal, creation, file access, rename, replacement, locking, tree verification and cleanup use directory-relative operations anchored to open descriptors and device/inode identities.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-fallback: Hosts lacking POSIX dir_fd operations, O_DIRECTORY or O_NOFOLLOW fail closed with RootedFilesystemError; there is no silent pathname fallback.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-adversarial-tests: Deterministic tests cover symlinked components, special entries, source-file and source-parent swaps, publication-root and pointer-parent swaps, lock-parent swaps and concurrent generation behavior without exposing replacement paths as successful publication.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-validation: 60 focused tests, the full 4021-test suite, Ruff, formatting, diff checks, maintainability, descriptor stress, real CLI publication/readback and all GitHub checks passed on head 706a397ba48ccea4e6271070d99e968eedae3d6a.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t025-nonclaims: No kernel-bug resistance, network-filesystem equivalence, privilege-boundary isolation, untested-platform security, distributed consensus, cross-host transactionality, remote freshness or semantic truth is claimed.
